vehicles.slot: Small round cap on top-left corner of shifter console — pry off to access slot
shift: Turn ignition to ON (do not start engine).
Locate the small round cap on the top-left corner of the gear selector console.
Pry the cap off with your ignition key or a small flathead.
Insert the key into the exposed slot and press down firmly.
While holding, press the brake pedal.
Move the shifter from Park to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Battery is under the hood — connect a jump pack.
Once power is restored, press brake and shift to Neutral normally.
If no power: use shift lock slot method above.
vehicles.notes: Key interlock recall Service Bulletin 05-009 affects some units — verify recall was completed.
vehicles.warn: Always apply parking brake and chock wheels before releasing Park on any incline.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear (front/drive wheels roll on ground) or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near the gear selector base on the center console, covered by a removable plastic cap
shift: Locate the plastic cap near the selector base.
Insert ignition key, press down firmly.
neutral: Connect jump pack to battery under hood.
Shift to Neutral normally once powered.
If no power: use slot method above.
vehicles.notes: 8-speed DCT: power strongly preferred. Flatbed tow only for DCT variants.
vehicles.warn: 8-speed DCT: FLATBED ONLY. Do not tow with wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear (front/drive wheels roll on ground) or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near the gear selector base, covered by a plastic cap
shift: Locate plastic cap near selector base.
vehicles.notes: Brake lights must be functioning for the normal shift interlock to work. If brake lights are out, use the manual slot.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

vehicles.slot: Slot on front-right side of shifter base — snap-off plastic cover
shift: Find the snap-off plastic cover on the front-right side of the shifter base.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

vehicles.slot: FRONT LIP of center console near the dashboard — silver rectangular slot. NOT near the shift handle.
shift: Look at the very front base of the center console, where it meets the dashboard — NOT at the shifter base.
Find the silver rectangular slot at the FRONT console lip.
Insert ignition key or small flathead into the slot.
Move the shifter from Park to Neutral.
neutral: Apply parking brake and chock wheels.
Connect jump pack to battery under hood — ZF 9HP is very hard to shift without power.
Once powered, press brake and shift normally.
If no power: use front console lip slot. A Honda/Acura ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 online) is the most reliable option.
vehicles.notes: CRITICAL: The slot is at the FRONT of the console near the dashboard, NOT at the shifter base — this is a very common mistake. Always carry a jump pack with ZF 9HP-equipped vehicles.
vehicles.warn: ZF 9HP: jump pack strongly recommended. Forcing shift without power risks transmission damage.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — SH-AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

knownIssues.title: ZF 9HP Shift Lock Requires Power
knownIssues.detail: The ZF 9HP 9-speed transmission requires 12V power to shift. Without power the standard slot method is very difficult. Always carry a jump pack. A dedicated ZF 9HP override tool (~$50 online) is the most reliable mechanical option.

knownIssues.title: Shift Lock Slot Location Misidentified
knownIssues.detail: The shift lock slot is at the FRONT lip of the center console near the dashboard — NOT near the shift handle base. Technicians and operators regularly probe the wrong location. Look at where the console meets the dash.

battery.warnings: ZF 9HP: must restore power before TC shift. Use jump pack rated for AGM batteries.

vehicles.slot: Near base of electronic gear selector on center console
shift: Press START button once without pressing brake (ON/electronics mode).
Locate shift lock slot near electronic selector base.
Insert key or flathead, press firmly.
neutral: Connect jump pack to battery under hood.
Press START without brake (ON mode), then press brake and shift to N.
Without power: use manual slot method.
vehicles.notes: Electronic selector design. Slot location may vary by trim — consult owner's manual.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — SH-AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near the gear selector base, covered by a plastic cap
shift: Find plastic cap near selector base. Pry off.
vehicles.notes: 2007-2009: AWD (SH-AWD) standard on all models. 2010-2012: FWD option added.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Slot on side of gear selector base near console
shift: Find slot on side of selector base.
vehicles.warn: AWD — use flatbed tow truck preferred.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Near base of the electronic push-button shifter on center console
shift: Press START once without brake (ACC/ON mode).
Find shift lock slot near electronic shifter base.
Insert key or flathead, press firmly.
Press the N button on the electronic shifter — vehicle is now in Neutral.
neutral: Press START without brake (ON mode), press brake, press N button.
Without power: shift lock slot method.
vehicles.notes: 3rd gen uses electronic push-button shifter — no physical lever. N button must be activated electronically.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base, covered by a cap
shift: Locate cap near selector base. Pry off.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — SH-AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base, covered by a cap
shift: Locate cap near selector base. Pry off.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Small round cap on upper-left of shifter base
shift: Find the small round cap on the upper-left of the shifter base. Pry off.
vehicles.notes: Manual transmission: no shift lock — depress clutch, select N. Key interlock recall SB 05-009.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear (front/drive wheels roll on ground) or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Slot on right side of shifter base, snap-off plastic cover
shift: Find snap-off cover on right side of shifter base. Remove.
vehicles.notes: SH-AWD available with 5-speed auto (2009-2011), 6-speed auto (2012-2014), or 6-speed manual (2010-2014). SH-AWD: flatbed only.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. SH-AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Slot at base of gear selector on console
shift: POWER METHOD: Press START twice without pressing brake (1st = radio, 2nd = all electronics on).
Find slot at selector base. Insert key or flathead, press firmly.
Press brake. Move selector to Neutral.
neutral: Press START once = ACC. Press START again = all electronics on.
Press brake and shift to Neutral.
If battery is dead: connect jump pack, then use power method above.
vehicles.notes: Power method preferred for 8-speed DCT. 9-speed (2018+): slot method works reliably.
vehicles.warn: 8-speed DCT: FLATBED ONLY. Do not tow with all four wheels on ground.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. SH-AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
DUAL-CLUTCH TRANSMISSION (DCT): Power is strongly preferred to shift to neutral. DCT without power may not release — use flatbed if shift override fails.

knownIssues.title: 8-Speed DCT Cannot Be Towed on Wheels
knownIssues.detail: 2015-2017 TLX with 8-speed DCT must be flatbedded. Towing with driven wheels on the ground will destroy the dual-clutch transmission. Always verify which transmission is installed before attempting wheel-lift.

battery.warnings: 8-speed DCT models: restore power before attempting shift

vehicles.slot: Near base of electronic gear selector on center console
shift: Press N on selector to go to Neutral.
Find slot near electronic selector base. Insert key, press firmly.
Press brake. Press N on selector.
neutral: Follow shift lock steps above once powered.
vehicles.towMethod: FWD: lift from rear — front (drive) wheels roll on ground. AWD: flatbed only. AWD without flatbed: wheel-lift with dollies on opposite axle. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.

vehicles.slot: Slot on right side of shifter base, snap-off cover
shift: Find snap-off cover on right side of shifter base. Remove.
vehicles.notes: Manual: no shift lock — depress clutch. Sport Wagon: same procedure.
vehicles.towMethod: Lift from rear (front/drive wheels roll on ground) or flatbed. Wheel-lift max: 35 mph, 50 miles — flatbed preferred for longer distances.
towNotes: FWD: Lift from REAR. Front (drive) wheels stay on the ground and roll freely. If rear-lift is not possible, place wheel dollies under the REAR wheels and tow front-first.
MANUAL TRANSMISSION OPTION: If equipped with manual trans, no shift lock — depress clutch and select Neutral. No override slot needed.

vehicles.slot: Small slot near gear selector base, covered by a cap
shift: Find cap near selector base. Pry off.
vehicles.towMethod: FLATBED ONLY — SH-AWD cannot be disabled. If no flatbed available, use wheel-lift with dollies on free-rolling axle.
towNotes: FULL-TIME AWD: Cannot disengage AWD. Flatbed is the safest method. If using wheel-lift, dollies MUST be used on the free-rolling axle to prevent driveline damage.
